topic.name: Student Loan Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming student loan processes, repayment options, and borrower protections.

Document 1: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow certain borrowers to consolidate their private education loans as Direct Consolidation Loans.
Directs the Secretary of Education to inform and educate students and their families about this loan consolidation program.
Document 2: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to establish two new repayment plan options.
Requires an institution of higher education that participates in federal student aid programs to provide information on the new IBR and fixed repayment plan options.
Document 3: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require institutions of higher education to provide pre-loan counseling.
Adds to the information to be provided to students as part of such counseling: an estimate of their projected loan debt-to-income ratio upon graduation.
Document 4: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to limit the unpaid amount of Direct Stafford loans that students may amass before completing their undergraduate education.
Caps the annual interest rate for Direct Stafford loans at 6.8%.
Document 5: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow certain borrowers to consolidate their private education loans as Direct Consolidation Loans.
Terminates the authority to make Consolidation loans under such program on June 30, 2012.
Document 6: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require a student loan borrower's prepayment amount to be applied first toward outstanding fees.
Requires a borrower's prepayment amount on a private education loan to be applied first toward outstanding fees.
Document 7: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to limit the unpaid amount of Direct Stafford loans that students may amass before completing their undergraduate education.
Directs the Secretary to charge the borrower of a Direct PLUS loan an origination fee of up to 3% of the loan principal.
Document 8: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require that when the holder of a borrower's loans receives a prepayment, it is to be applied first toward any fees due.
Requires an institution of higher education to apply any excess payments by the borrower toward any fees due on such loans.
Document 9: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to set the annual interest rate on Direct Loans at the bond equivalent rate on 91-day Treasury bills.
Requires institutions of higher education to provide student borrowers of title IV loans with information regarding their options for loan consolidation.
Document 10: Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to expand lender disclosure requirements.
Requires an IHE to provide pre-loan counseling to a student borrower at or prior to the first disbursement of each new loan.

topic.name: Labor Organization Elections
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations surrounding the election processes for labor organizations and the rights of employees in collective bargaining representation.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to make it an unlawful labor practice for a labor organization or its agents to interfere with the rights of employees to organize and select representation to collectively bargain.
Requires the NLRB to give 14 days advance notice before a hearing when it is investigating an election petition.
Document 5: Revises the requirement that the NLRB direct an election by secret ballot, and certify its results, whenever it finds upon the record of such a hearing that a question of representation exists.
Prohibits an election less than 40 calendar days following the filing of an election petition.
Document 10: Requires an employer to provide the NLRB a list of employee names and home addresses of all eligible voters within 7 days following the NLRB's determination of the appropriate unit.

Prohibits an election from taking place after the filing of a petition unless and until: (1) a hearing is conducted before a qualified hearing officer; and (2) the issues are resolved by a regional Director.

topic.name: Electric Vehicle Deployment and Fuel Efficiency
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation aimed at promoting electric vehicle adoption, enhancing fuel efficiency standards, and reducing oil consumption in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to develop a fuel efficiency program for passenger car and light truck tires.
Requires a specified graduated percentage of passenger cars manufactured by light-duty motor vehicles manufacturers to be fuel choice enabling motor vehicles or alternative fuel automobiles.
Document 2: Revises corporate average fuel economy standards (CAFE Standards) provisions for automobiles and light trucks.
Establishes a national tire fuel economy program for tires designed for use on passenger cars and light trucks.
Document 3: Establishes within the Department of Energy (DOE) a national plug-in electric drive vehicle deployment program.
Directs the Secretary of Energy to provide technical assistance upon request to state, local, and tribal governments with the deployment of plug-in electric drive vehicles.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a two-phased competitive program to provide financial assistance to states for the deployment of electric drive vehicles.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for qualified electric vehicle refueling property bonds.
Document 5: Establishes a national energy security program to reduce oil consumption by calendar 2030.
Directs the Secretary of Transportation and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to promulgate joint regulations establishing fuel efficiency standards.
Document 6: Instructs the Secretary of Transportation to prescribe average heavy duty vehicle fuel economy standards.
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a credit for advanced motor vehicle technology expenses.
Document 7: Revises the Corporate Average Fuel Economy (CAFE) standards by requiring at least a 4% annual increase in the average fuel economy level.
Requires manufacturers to ensure that 50% of automobiles and light duty trucks manufactured in model years 2013-2014 are dual fueled.
Document 8: Establishes a national energy security program to coordinate federal activities to reduce oil consumption by 8 million barrels per day by calendar 2030.
Directs the Secretary of Energy to develop a national plan for electric drive vehicle deployment.
Document 9: Establishes a national electric drive vehicle deployment program and a targeted electric drive vehicle deployment communities program.
Directs the Secretary to develop and publish guidance for model building codes for electric vehicle charging infrastructure.
Document 10: Establishes within the Department of Energy (DOE) a national electric drive vehicle deployment program.
Allows a refundable personal credit for a qualified deployment community taxpayer.

topic.name: Medicare Program Integrity and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving the integrity, efficiency, and effectiveness of the Medicare program, focusing on payment reforms, fraud prevention, and beneficiary protections.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary to study and report to Congress on the use of technology to provide real-time data analysis of Medicare reimbursement claims to identify and investigate unusual billing or order practices under the Medicare program that could indicate fraud or abuse.
Requires the Secretary to establish procedures to require carriers... to confirm with the National Supplier Clearinghouse.
Document 2: establish a five-year, population-based payment demonstration project through which provider networks are prospectively paid monthly capitated payments for coordinated care furnished to Medicare beneficiaries.
CMS shall establish a process for prospectively assigning Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to a participating provider network.
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to... negotiate with pharmaceutical manufacturers to reduce the purchase cost of covered part D drugs.
Sets forth anti-fraud and abuse provisions.
Document 4: Directs the Medicare Payment Advisory Commission (MEDPAC) to report to Congress on the relationship between... physician and other health professional utilization and expenditures...
establish an update of 1% for health professionals participating in alternative payment models (APMs) and an update of 0.5% for all other health professionals after 2023.
Document 5: revise requirements for payments for physician's services to eliminate the sustainable growth rate system from the formula for determination of the fee schedules for such services.
Directs the Secretary, through the Center for Medicare and Medicaid Innovation, to... expand testing of each payment and service delivery model.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish cost-effective procedures to ensure that... a Social Security account number... is not displayed... on the Medicare card.
Requires each Medicare administrative contractor to establish an improper payment outreach and education program for service providers and suppliers.
Document 8: Requires the Secretary's annual report to Congress on the use of recovery audit contractors under the Medicare Integrity Program to... assess the effectiveness of changes made to Medicare payment policies and procedures in order to address those vulnerabilities.
Requires the Secretary to address improper payment vulnerabilities in a timely manner.
Document 9: Requires the Secretary's annual report to Congress on the use of recovery audit contractors under the Medicare Integrity Program to... describe the types and financial cost of improper payment vulnerabilities identified by recovery audit contractors.
Directs the Secretary to develop a plan to revise the incentive program under the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 for the reporting of fraud and abuse.

topic.name: Gang Activity and Violent Crime Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at combating gang-related activities and violent crimes through enhanced penalties and law enforcement measures.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to modify and expand prohibitions against criminal street gang activity
Expands the rebuttable presumption against release of persons charged with firearms offenses.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to modify and expand prohibitions against criminal street gang activity
Sets a 15-year statute of limitations for non-capital felony crimes of violence.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to define 'criminal street gang' and 'gang crime'
Increases criminal penalties for certain violent crimes in aid of racketeering activity.
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code to increase criminal penalties for transferring firearms for use in crimes of violence or drug trafficking
Expand criteria for the detention for persons charged with firearms offenses prior to trial.
Document 5: Impose mandatory minimum prison terms for violent crimes committed to further racketeering activities
Directs the Attorney General to establish and assist anti-gang task forces.
Document 6: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to increase prison terms for manufacturing, distributing, dispensing, or possessing certain large amounts of drugs
Directs the Attorney General to develop a National Drug Trafficking Enforcement Strategy.
Document 7: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to increase prison terms for drug trafficking crimes
Directs the Attorney General to develop a National Drug Trafficking Enforcement Strategy.
Document 8: Amends the federal criminal code to increase or impose new criminal penalties for the use of firearms to commit a crime of violence
Extends the limitation period for prosecuting violent crimes and crimes of terrorism.
Document 9: Increases criminal penalties for violent crimes and drug crimes committed by illegal aliens
Imposes mandatory restitution for victims of violent crime.
Document 10: Defines 'criminal street gang' and 'gang crime' and prohibits the commission of gang crimes
Establishes a grant program for crime prevention, research, and intervention services designed for gang members.

topic.name: Iran and Syria Sanctions
topic.description: The documents focus on U.S. legislative measures imposing sanctions on Iran and Syria to counter nuclear proliferation, human rights abuses, and support for terrorism.

Document 2: Subjects Iran to certain economic sanctions.
Prohibits direct or indirect importation of Iranian articles into the United States.
Document 3: Expresses the sense of Congress that Iran must not be allowed to develop or maintain nuclear weapon capabilities.
States that it is U.S. policy to seek to ensure that all countries reduce their purchases of crude oil from Iran.

Document 8: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on a compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.
temporary suspension of duty on compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.
Document 9: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend temporarily the suspension of duty on compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.
temporarily extend the suspension of duty on compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.
Document 10: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.
temporary suspension of duty on compound of strontium chloroapatite-europium.

topic.name: Aviation Safety and Security Regulations
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislation aimed at enhancing safety, security, and technological advancements in the aviation industry.

Document 1: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration to issue regulations to ensure that a covered aircraft is equipped with technology that provides for the continuous tracking of information regarding the aircraft during flight.

work collaboratively with the International Civil Aviation Organization and other relevant stakeholders to develop and implement international standards that improve the tracking of aircraft in flight.
Document 2: This bill directs the Department of Transportation (DOT) to require domestic or foreign air carriers and manufacturers of aircraft to disclose to the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) any attempted or successful cyberattack against any system on board an aircraft.
the FAA shall use the information obtained through such disclosures to improve the regulations to incorporate requirements relating to cybersecurity.
Document 3: Requires a pilot of an aircraft providing emergency medical services to comply with federal safety operating requirements governing commuter and on demand operations.
Requires the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to initiate a rulemaking to establish a standardized checklist of risk evaluation factors.
Document 4: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) to require all commercial passenger aircraft ordered by an air carrier on or after January 1, 2016, to be equipped with a deployable recorder system.
Defines 'commercial passenger aircraft' to mean a jet aircraft with a maximum certificated take-off mass over 15,000 kilograms.
Document 7: Directs the Assistant Secretary of Homeland Security (Transportation Security Administration) to implement a pilot program at five commercial service airports to physically screen all airport workers with access to sterile areas of the airport.
Requires, with respect to the pilot program, screening to be conducted under the same standards as apply to individuals at airport security screening checkpoints.
Document 9: Extends funding for aviation security.
Provides for a system to screen all cargo transported on domestic and foreign passenger airlines.

Document 1: Reauthorizes the Clean Water Act State Revolving Fund (SRF)

Document 2: Makes livestock indemnity payments (at 65% of an animal's market value)

Document 3: Prohibits any direct, counter-cyclical or transition payment to an individual or entity whose adjusted gross income exceeds $200,000.

Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to make FY2012 livestock indemnity payments

Document 5: Establishes the agriculture risk coverage program through crop year 2018

Document 6: Establishes the agriculture risk coverage program for crop years 2014-2018

Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to provide for a farm's base acreage adjustment

Document 8: Repeals direct payments under the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008

Document 9: Requires election of either direct payments and counter-cyclical payments

Document 10: Defines specified terms.
Requires a reduction in base acres if the sum of base acres and other specified acreage exceeds the actual cropland acreage on the farm.

topic.name: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension
topic.description: This topic covers legislative acts aimed at extending emergency unemployment compensation benefits for eligible individuals during times of economic distress.

Document 1: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2014 - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008, (SSA, 2008) to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before July 1, 2014.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until June 30, 2014, requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.
Document 2: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 (SAA, 2008) to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before June 1, 2014.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until May 31, 2014, requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.
Document 3: Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2008 - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to revise the formula for Tier-1 amounts a state credits to an applicant's emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA) for a benefit year.
Extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation.
Document 4: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2013 - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before January 1, 2015.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until December 31, 2014, requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.
Document 5: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 (SSA, 2008) to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before April 1, 2014.
Reauthorizes Tier-1, Tier-2, Tier-3, and Tier-4 of the EUC program for weeks ending after December 29, 2013.
Document 6: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Expansion Act of 2011 - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Extends the program until September 22, 2012.
Document 7: Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2008 - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to revise the formula for Tier-1 amounts a state credits to an applicant's emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA) for a benefit year.
Extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation through August 27, 2009.
Document 8: Emergency Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2014 - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008, (SSA, 2008) to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before July 1, 2014.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until June 30, 2014, requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.
Document 9: Unemployment Compensation Extension Act of 2008 - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to revise the formula for Tier-1 amounts a state credits to an applicant's emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA) for a benefit year.
Extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation.
Document 10: Americans Want to Work Act - Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account (EUCA).
Requires a further additional Tier-5 period for deposits to an individual's EUCA.

Document 6: Amends federal bankruptcy law governing a Chapter 13 debtor

the identity of the caller, and the appearance of the caller's telephone number on the recipient's caller identification service
